evals.prompt: Analyze https://example.com/products/widget, rank keyword opportunities, and recommend improved search and social metadata in the default report location.
evals.expected_output: Announces the seven-call budget, audits current metadata, derives five seeds from qualifying primary content, makes five bounded Related Keywords calls, and writes three coherent packages under SEO/example.com/.
assertions: The workflow states a normal budget of two OnPage calls and five Related Keywords calls before execution.
Exactly five seeds are justified solely by qualifying Content Parsing main-topic headings and primary text.
Each Related Keywords call uses the same selected market, depth 2, limit 25, and descending search-volume order.
The report ranks at most 20 rows with the documented opportunity_proxy and labels it analyst-derived rather than a DataForSEO metric.
The report contains three coherent search/social metadata packages and marks one recommended.

evals.prompt: Create page metadata suggestions and a keyword report for my landing page.
evals.expected_output: Asks for the missing absolute HTTP(S) page URL and waits without making a billable request or creating a report.
assertions: The workflow asks for one absolute HTTP(S) page URL and waits.
No DataForSEO call or report is produced before the URL is supplied.

evals.prompt: Run seo-page-metadata for https://www.example.de/leistungen/seo?customer=acme using Germany and de, and save it under ./client-reports.
evals.expected_output: Preserves the query for the page request, redacts it in the report, normalizes example.de for the directory, respects Germany/de, and creates a safe filename that does not expose the query value.
assertions: Both OnPage calls use the validated URL including its query and without a fragment.
The report Scope redacts query and fragment contents.
All five Related Keywords calls use Germany and de.
The report path is under the cwd-resolved client-reports/example.de directory.
The filename omits customer and acme, includes a deterministic hash, and is no longer than 140 characters.

evals.prompt: Analyze https://example.org/lahore/mobile-vet. The current title says online pet store, while the parsed main content offers mobile veterinary visits throughout Lahore.
evals.expected_output: Audits the incorrect metadata without using it as evidence, derives veterinary seeds from main-topic content, resolves Lahore to Pakistan, and documents the market source.
assertions: The pet-store metadata is reported but excluded from topic, intent, geography, seed, and claim derivation.
Exactly five seeds are grounded in mobile-veterinary main-topic evidence and use Lahore only where natural.
Pakistan is used only because Lahore is an explicit and unambiguous primary-content service area.
The report identifies primary content as the country source.

evals.prompt: Create metadata for https://example.com/consulting. Main content says the consultancy serves manufacturers across Ontario, Canada; Toronto appears only in the footer office address.
evals.expected_output: Uses Ontario/Canada as service geography, rejects footer-only Toronto as market evidence, and uses the content language independently.
assertions: Ontario or Canada is incorporated naturally into applicable seeds.
Toronto is explicitly rejected as target geography because it appears only in the footer.
All Related Keywords calls use Canada when there is no user country override.
Country and language sources are reported separately.

evals.prompt: Analyze https://example.net/services/mobile-vet for Germany and de. Main content says the service operates only in Lahore, Pakistan.
evals.expected_output: Respects Germany/de for keyword calls, keeps suggestions grounded in Lahore service content, and discloses the market/geography conflict.
assertions: All five Related Keywords calls use the explicit Germany and de override.
Seeds and suggested claims remain grounded in Lahore primary content.
The report discloses the conflict between requested keyword market and content-defined service geography.

evals.prompt: Analyze https://example.com/thin-page. Content Parsing returns only a cookie banner, navigation links, and two generic words in main_topic.
evals.expected_output: Stops after the OnPage evidence gate because five distinct seeds cannot be justified, reports completed costs, and does not call Related Keywords.
assertions: Cookie and navigation content are excluded from positive evidence.
The workflow does not invent or pad five seeds.
No Related Keywords call is made.
The diagnostic names completed calls, costs, and the evidence shortfall.

evals.prompt: Analyze https://example.com/app. The non-JavaScript Content Parsing response is successful but has no main_topic content; the page is known to render client-side.
evals.expected_output: Stops before keyword calls and asks permission for one potentially more expensive JavaScript-rendered retry.
assertions: JavaScript is not enabled on the initial OnPage calls.
No Related Keywords call occurs without qualifying content.
The workflow asks before retrying with enable_javascript true and explains the cost implication.

evals.prompt: Run the metadata workflow for https://example.com/service. MCP transport succeeds, but the Content Parsing provider task returns status 40501 and no result.
evals.expected_output: Treats the task failure as a failure rather than empty content, does not make keyword calls, and asks before any eligible retry.
assertions: The nested task status and message are validated and reported.
The missing result is not treated as an empty successful page.
No Related Keywords call or complete report is produced from the failed evidence call.

evals.prompt: Related Keywords returns Emergency Vet Lahore twice with Unicode whitespace from two seeds, volume 0 and CPC 0, plus one conflicting non-null volume; another keyword has null KD.
evals.expected_output: Normalizes and merges the duplicate, preserves zero, records provenance and the conflicting volume, and excludes the null-KD row from opportunity ranking without converting null to zero.
assertions: Unicode NFKC, whitespace collapse, and casefolding produce one Emergency Vet Lahore row.
Both source seeds are preserved.
Volume 0 and CPC 0 remain zero.
The first non-null conflicting metric is retained and the alternate value is logged.
Null KD remains null and the row is counted as metric-incomplete.

evals.prompt: Analyze a Spanish-language page with no explicit service country and no user market. The active MCP exposes no DataForSEO Labs locations-and-languages utility, and support for United States/es is uncertain.
evals.expected_output: Keeps Spanish as content/report language but asks for a supported keyword country/language choice before the five Related Keywords calls.
assertions: Country and language are resolved independently.
The workflow does not silently replace Spanish with English.
The workflow does not bypass MCP or guess an uncertain location/language pair.
No Related Keywords call is made before clarification.

evals.prompt: Analyze https://example.com/service and include legacy meta keywords in the report.
evals.expected_output: Adds one legacy meta-keywords list to each coherent package because it was explicitly requested, while clearly stating that Google and Bing do not use the tag for rankings.
assertions: Legacy meta keywords appear only because the user explicitly requested them.
They are labeled legacy-only and are not presented as an SEO ranking recommendation.
The normal search and social package recommendation remains complete without relying on meta keywords.

evals.prompt: Analyze https://example.com/products/widget and create the metadata report, but Python 3 is unavailable in this environment.
evals.expected_output: Continues with DataForSEO MCP and applies the complete validation, extraction, normalization, ranking, counting, cost, and safe-path contract directly instead of stopping for Python.
assertions: The workflow does not stop or ask the user to install Python solely because Python 3 is unavailable.
URL validation, query and fragment handling, report redaction, and safe deterministic filename rules remain enforced.
Response validation, null-versus-zero preservation, deduplication, conflict logging, opportunity ranking, Unicode code-point counts, and cost arithmetic follow the documented contract.
The report method and limitations identify that the prompt-native fallback was used.
